author: omerfirmak
content:
there seems to be a slight difference between the EIP and spec. 

spec (https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/eips/amsterdam/eip-7928/src/ethereum/forks/amsterdam/vm/instructions/environment.py#L397-L402) charges the whole total cost of the opcode (`access_gas_cost + copy_gas_cost + extend_memory.cost`) before accessing the target address, but EIP (https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/blob/master/EIPS/eip-7928.md?plain=1#L147) requires only `access_cost + memory_expansion` to be charged before the state access.
